name: Cyan, Inc. v. Beaver County Employees' Retirement Fund
question: Do state courts have subject matter jurisdiction over covered class actions that allege only Securities Act of 1933 claims?
description: A case in which the Court held that that the Securities Litigation Uniform Standards Act of 1998 does not strip state courts of jurisdiction to hear claims under the Securities Act of 1933, nor does it permit defendants to remove class actions alleging only 1933 Act claims from state to federal court.
